{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NvFx4bQhMf8xVZpL84Skvn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"652f2cb5-5d7e-4b87-a233-9f6076adbb68","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:08.241Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GCbYWLZ18i4zyzyKDANoSV","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## Working Directory\n    53→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    54→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    55→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    56→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"53b5fddd-ac10-4e47-a57c-a78626e0e916","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:08.630Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":56,"startLine":1,"totalLines":56}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01XQX9ZNwG8tJ3ANbcmStP8e","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GZCvbdki3sWm7UkUTg2R8o","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue list --state all --limit 50 --json number,title,state,labels 2>/dev/null | python3 -c \"import json,sys; issues=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(f'{i[\\\"number\\\"]:3d} {i[\\\"state\\\"]:6s} {\\\",\\\".join(l[\\\"name\\\"] for l in i[\\\"labels\\\"]):30s} {i[\\\"title\\\"]}') for i in issues]\"","description":"List all issues with labels"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3378,"cache_read_input_tokens":13766,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3378},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"545e1b6d-2b81-49b2-91a2-68992e0ea216"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MbC9XXMJWekfu7gxmVqxEg","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Decisions Log\n     2→\n     3→## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n     4→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n     5→- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n     6→- No feature loss for our use case\n     7→\n     8→## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n     9→- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n    10→- Simple, no external dependencies\n    11→- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n    12→\n    13→## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n    14→- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n    15→- No separate session store needed\n    16→- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n    17→\n    18→## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n    19→- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n    20→- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection\n    21→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"5c0c3d9f-ce7c-4e9b-9870-2d871f993d0e","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:31.540Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/decisions.md","content":"# Decisions Log\n\n## 2026-03-19: Next.js 15 over 16\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root directory)\n- Downgraded to Next.js 15 which uses webpack by default\n- No feature loss for our use case\n\n## 2026-03-19: SQLite for MVP\n- Per PRD, using SQLite with file storage in app/data/\n- Simple, no external dependencies\n- Good enough for MVP; can migrate to Postgres later if needed\n\n## 2026-03-19: iron-session for auth\n- Lightweight session management with encrypted httpOnly cookies\n- No separate session store needed\n- Password hashing with bcrypt (12 rounds)\n\n## 2026-03-19: Session logs gitignored\n- Session logs contain raw API data that may include tokens\n- Added to .gitignore to prevent secret leaks via GitHub push protection\n","numLines":21,"startLine":1,"totalLines":21}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MXTjninpyqbSSFByg38XCm","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session 2026-03-20 17:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## What was found\n     4→- Production healthy: PM2 online (57min uptime, 0 restarts), HTTP 200\n     5→- PR #39 (production deploy) already merged\n     6→- All PRD issues closed, no open PRs\n     7→- Only open issue: #21 (needs-human, Telegram bot token) — XanderZhu commented asking for deploy first\n     8→- No health check endpoint existed\n     9→- Default Next.js 404 page (not branded)\n    10→- No Open Graph meta tags on listing pages\n    11→\n    12→## What was decided and why\n    13→- Responded to issue #21 with deployment details (app is now live, webhook URL provided)\n    14→- Implemented production-readiness improvements: health endpoint, custom 404, OG/Twitter meta tags\n    15→- These are standard production hardening that improve monitoring, UX, and social sharing\n    16→\n    17→## What was done\n    18→1. Replied to issue #21 with deployment URL and Telegram webhook setup instructions\n    19→2. Created `/api/health` endpoint — returns DB connectivity, uptime, user/listing counts (503 on failure)\n    20→3. Created custom `not-found.tsx` — branded 404 page with \"Browse listings\" CTA\n    21→4. Refactored listing detail page: split into server page.tsx (generateMetadata) + client ListingDetail.tsx\n    22→5. Added OG + Twitter Card meta tags to listing pages (title, author, cover image, description)\n    23→6. Added base OG metadata to root layout\n    24→7. Built, linted, tested locally — all pass\n    25→8. Created issue #40, committed, pushed, opened PR #41\n    26→9. CI passed, merged PR #41, deployed to production\n    27→10. Verified all three features in production\n    28→\n    29→## What was encountered\n    30→- Git remote lost PAT between sessions (known pattern) — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    31→\n    32→## What was learned\n    33→- No new patterns — session went smoothly following existing knowledge\n    34→\n    35→## Tools & skills used\n    36→- **Read**: PRD, memory files, source code (layout, API routes, listing page)\n    37→- **Glob**: memory files, session logs, not-found page check\n    38→- **Grep**: Telegram webhook files, export patterns, OG/Twitter tags verification\n    39→- **Bash**: PM2 status, curl health checks, git operations, build/lint, deploy, GitHub CLI\n    40→- **Write**: health endpoint, not-found page, listing page.tsx\n    41→- **Edit**: ListingDetail rename, layout OG metadata\n    42→- **agent-browser**: Production homepage/login screenshots\n    43→- **session-completion**: Session wrap-up procedure\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"f4f82f24-3ad9-43a5-9b84-df6fb6f2438a","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:41.091Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/sessions/session-2026-03-20_17-00.md","content":"# Session 2026-03-20 17:00 UTC\n\n## What was found\n- Production healthy: PM2 online (57min uptime, 0 restarts), HTTP 200\n- PR #39 (production deploy) already merged\n- All PRD issues closed, no open PRs\n- Only open issue: #21 (needs-human, Telegram bot token) — XanderZhu commented asking for deploy first\n- No health check endpoint existed\n- Default Next.js 404 page (not branded)\n- No Open Graph meta tags on listing pages\n\n## What was decided and why\n- Responded to issue #21 with deployment details (app is now live, webhook URL provided)\n- Implemented production-readiness improvements: health endpoint, custom 404, OG/Twitter meta tags\n- These are standard production hardening that improve monitoring, UX, and social sharing\n\n## What was done\n1. Replied to issue #21 with deployment URL and Telegram webhook setup instructions\n2. Created `/api/health` endpoint — returns DB connectivity, uptime, user/listing counts (503 on failure)\n3. Created custom `not-found.tsx` — branded 404 page with \"Browse listings\" CTA\n4. Refactored listing detail page: split into server page.tsx (generateMetadata) + client ListingDetail.tsx\n5. Added OG + Twitter Card meta tags to listing pages (title, author, cover image, description)\n6. Added base OG metadata to root layout\n7. Built, linted, tested locally — all pass\n8. Created issue #40, committed, pushed, opened PR #41\n9. CI passed, merged PR #41, deployed to production\n10. Verified all three features in production\n\n## What was encountered\n- Git remote lost PAT between sessions (known pattern) — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n\n## What was learned\n- No new patterns — session went smoothly following existing knowledge\n\n## Tools & skills used\n- **Read**: PRD, memory files, source code (layout, API routes, listing page)\n- **Glob**: memory files, session logs, not-found page check\n- **Grep**: Telegram webhook files, export patterns, OG/Twitter tags verification\n- **Bash**: PM2 status, curl health checks, git operations, build/lint, deploy, GitHub CLI\n- **Write**: health endpoint, not-found page, listing page.tsx\n- **Edit**: ListingDetail rename, layout OG metadata\n- **agent-browser**: Production homepage/login screenshots\n- **session-completion**: Session wrap-up procedure\n","numLines":44,"startLine":1,"totalLines":44}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GrEvLg4G7oidHead2uXbrC","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→`);\n   142→\n   143→// Rating and Reputation system\n   144→db.exec(`\n   145→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   146→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   147→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   148→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   149→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   150→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   151→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   152→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   153→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   154→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   155→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   156→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   157→  );\n   158→`);\n   159→\n   160→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   161→db.exec(`\n   162→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   163→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   164→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   165→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   166→`);\n   167→\n   168→export default db;\n   169→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"c40f5099-99cb-472c-9689-814262d40e27","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:20.915Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Eh1vqWZecf9twxGPtyTf3S","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"5069271e-3c60-466b-83e2-3c02ba8143a9","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:21.298Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RX5s7tREgkxTwPULMMwAJy","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import db from \"./db\";\n     2→import { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n     3→\n     4→function getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n     5→  const user = db\n     6→    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n     7→    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n     8→  return user?.email || null;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export function createNotification(\n    12→  userId: number,\n    13→  listingId: number,\n    14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n    20→\n    21→  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n    22→  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n    23→  if (email) {\n    24→    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    25→    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n    26→      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    27→    });\n    28→  }\n    29→}\n    30→\n    31→function getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n    32→  switch (type) {\n    33→    case \"new_member\":\n    34→      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    35→    case \"group_full\":\n    36→      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    37→    case \"application_received\":\n    38→      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    39→    case \"application_approved\":\n    40→      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    41→    case \"application_rejected\":\n    42→      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    43→    case \"rating_received\":\n    44→      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    45→    default:\n    46→      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    47→  }\n    48→}\n    49→\n    50→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    51→  listingId: number,\n    52→  joinerName: string\n    53→) {\n    54→  const listing = db\n    55→    .prepare(\n    56→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    57→    )\n    58→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    59→\n    60→  if (listing) {\n    61→    createNotification(\n    62→      listing.author_id,\n    63→      listingId,\n    64→      \"new_member\",\n    65→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    66→    );\n    67→  }\n    68→}\n    69→\n    70→export function notifyApplicationReceived(\n    71→  listingId: number,\n    72→  applicantName: string\n    73→) {\n    74→  const listing = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    77→    )\n    78→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    79→\n    80→  if (listing) {\n    81→    createNotification(\n    82→      listing.author_id,\n    83→      listingId,\n    84→      \"application_received\",\n    85→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    86→    );\n    87→  }\n    88→}\n    89→\n    90→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    91→  userId: number,\n    92→  listingId: number,\n    93→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    94→) {\n    95→  const listing = db\n    96→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    97→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n    98→\n    99→  if (listing) {\n   100→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   101→    const message =\n   102→      decision === \"approved\"\n   103→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   104→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   105→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→\n   109→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   110→  const listing = db\n   111→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   112→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   113→  const members = db\n   114→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   115→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   116→\n   117→  if (listing) {\n   118→    const botConfigured = !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   119→    const message = botConfigured\n   120→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The Telegram group will be set up automatically.`\n   121→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a Telegram link soon.`;\n   122→\n   123→    for (const member of members) {\n   124→      createNotification(\n   125→        member.user_id,\n   126→        listingId,\n   127→        \"group_full\",\n   128→        message\n   129→      );\n   130→    }\n   131→  }\n   132→}\n   133→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"1dc67011-3057-42f6-be80-b46b45c0a12c","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:21.462Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JW2hq91TXBWmCCXQ9f1fTq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     5→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     6→\n     7→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n     8→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n     9→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    10→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    11→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    12→\n    13→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    14→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    15→\n    16→  if (q) {\n    17→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    18→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    22→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    23→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    24→  }\n    25→\n    26→  if (readingPace) {\n    27→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    28→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  if (startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom)) {\n    32→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    33→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    37→\n    38→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    39→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    40→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    41→\n    42→  const listings = db\n    43→    .prepare(\n    44→      `SELECT\n    45→        l.*,\n    46→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    47→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    48→      FROM listings l\n    49→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    50→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    51→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}`\n    52→    )\n    53→    .all(...params);\n    54→\n    55→  return NextResponse.json({ listings });\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"dcc773ae-d602-4540-b380-25c35739d883","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:24.432Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D18D9uHAQqQvyvDasvEMAG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  try {\n    12→    const body = await req.json();\n    13→    const {\n    14→      bookTitle,\n    15→      bookAuthor,\n    16→      bookCoverUrl,\n    17→      bookOlid,\n    18→      language,\n    19→      readingPace,\n    20→      startDate,\n    21→      meetingFormat,\n    22→      maxGroupSize,\n    23→      requiresApproval,\n    24→      platformPreference,\n    25→    } = body;\n    26→\n    27→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    28→      return NextResponse.json(\n    29→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    30→        { status: 400 }\n    31→      );\n    32→    }\n    33→\n    34→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    35→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    36→      return NextResponse.json(\n    37→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    38→        { status: 400 }\n    39→      );\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    43→      return NextResponse.json(\n    44→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    45→        { status: 400 }\n    46→      );\n    47→    }\n    48→\n    49→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    50→      return NextResponse.json(\n    51→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    52→        { status: 400 }\n    53→      );\n    54→    }\n    55→\n    56→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    57→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json(\n    59→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    60→        { status: 400 }\n    61→      );\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    65→      ? platformPreference\n    66→      : \"telegram\";\n    67→\n    68→    const result = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    71→         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    72→      )\n    73→      .run(\n    74→        session.userId,\n    75→        bookTitle.trim(),\n    76→        bookAuthor.trim(),\n    77→        bookCoverUrl || \"\",\n    78→        bookOlid || \"\",\n    79→        language || \"English\",\n    80→        readingPace.trim(),\n    81→        startDate,\n    82→        meetingFormat,\n    83→        size,\n    84→        requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n    85→        platform\n    86→      );\n    87→\n    88→    // Auto-join the author as a member\n    89→    db.prepare(\n    90→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    91→    ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n    92→\n    93→    return NextResponse.json(\n    94→      { id: result.lastInsertRowid },\n    95→      { status: 201 }\n    96→    );\n    97→  } catch (error) {\n    98→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n    99→    return NextResponse.json(\n   100→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   101→      { status: 500 }\n   102→    );\n   103→  }\n   104→}\n   105→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"72de00ef-c12a-4cb9-97a3-686dce0f85c2","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:24.821Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QNMwNZ9XixsaShBQyNj1o7","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    57→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    58→    const { count } = db\n    59→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    60→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    61→\n    62→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    63→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    64→    }\n    65→\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    71→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    72→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    76→  });\n    77→\n    78→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    79→\n    80→  if (result.error) {\n    81→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    85→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    86→\n    87→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    88→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    89→  }\n    90→\n    91→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    92→}\n    93→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"3da9099f-e454-41cb-9583-fdf175bb922d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:25.239Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a1f5e46afabb2fbba","tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","description":"Reading app/api/auth/register/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":17174,"tool_uses":11,"duration_ms":21907},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"0196b23f-9c05-4e13-afe1-66b17a5db618","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019SLdUvHs593EmVbEwQAJdZ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  try {\n     8→    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n     9→\n    10→    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n    11→      return NextResponse.json(\n    12→        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n    13→        { status: 400 }\n    14→      );\n    15→    }\n    16→\n    17→    if (password.length < 8) {\n    18→      return NextResponse.json(\n    19→        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n    20→        { status: 400 }\n    21→      );\n    22→    }\n    23→\n    24→    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    25→    if (password.length > 72) {\n    26→      return NextResponse.json(\n    27→        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n    28→        { status: 400 }\n    29→      );\n    30→    }\n    31→\n    32→    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    33→      return NextResponse.json(\n    34→        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n    35→        { status: 400 }\n    36→      );\n    37→    }\n    38→\n    39→    if (email.length > 254) {\n    40→      return NextResponse.json(\n    41→        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n    42→        { status: 400 }\n    43→      );\n    44→    }\n    45→\n    46→    // Check if email is already taken\n    47→    const existing = db\n    48→      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    49→      .get(email);\n    50→    if (existing) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n    53→        { status: 409 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n    58→\n    59→    const result = db\n    60→      .prepare(\n    61→        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    62→      )\n    63→      .run(email.toLowerCase().trim(), passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n    64→\n    65→    // Auto-login after registration\n    66→    const session = await getSession();\n    67→    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    68→    session.email = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    69→    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    70→    await session.save();\n    71→\n    72→    return NextResponse.json(\n    73→      {\n    74→        user: {\n    75→          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n    76→          email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),\n    77→          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n    78→        },\n    79→      },\n    80→      { status: 201 }\n    81→    );\n    82→  } catch (error) {\n    83→    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n    84→    return NextResponse.json(\n    85→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    86→      { status: 500 }\n    87→    );\n    88→  }\n    89→}\n    90→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"4e6c2f70-3944-49a2-8a60-f3b1851d6089","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:29.964Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01M2DmN7WVvF7fxRpPEdpk8e","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01BQxVDQwUtXvgi9YHo28bVM","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/login/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3427,"cache_read_input_tokens":13466,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3427,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"61d1008c-d58e-4c1b-9e18-9323295ed5a6"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BQxVDQwUtXvgi9YHo28bVM","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  try {\n     8→    const { email, password } = await req.json();\n     9→\n    10→    if (!email || !password) {\n    11→      return NextResponse.json(\n    12→        { error: \"Email and password are required\" },\n    13→        { status: 400 }\n    14→      );\n    15→    }\n    16→\n    17→    const user = db\n    18→      .prepare(\"SELECT id, email, password_hash, display_name FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    19→      .get(email.toLowerCase().trim()) as\n    20→      | { id: number; email: string; password_hash: string; display_name: string }\n    21→      | undefined;\n    22→\n    23→    if (!user) {\n    24→      return NextResponse.json(\n    25→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    26→        { status: 401 }\n    27→      );\n    28→    }\n    29→\n    30→    const valid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password_hash);\n    31→    if (!valid) {\n    32→      return NextResponse.json(\n    33→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    34→        { status: 401 }\n    35→      );\n    36→    }\n    37→\n    38→    const session = await getSession();\n    39→    session.userId = user.id;\n    40→    session.email = user.email;\n    41→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    42→    await session.save();\n    43→\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({\n    45→      user: {\n    46→        id: user.id,\n    47→        email: user.email,\n    48→        displayName: user.display_name,\n    49→      },\n    50→    });\n    51→  } catch (error) {\n    52→    console.error(\"Login error:\", error);\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    55→      { status: 500 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"5a755c1c-c08c-4ac7-af7b-eabdb527b011","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:30.050Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01M2DmN7WVvF7fxRpPEdpk8e","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018vP6pVadtWkEQHZJrV8Lzn","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3427,"cache_read_input_tokens":13466,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3427,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"e08640b9-98d8-466e-b0c5-bfb53e63ca46"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018vP6pVadtWkEQHZJrV8Lzn","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     6→\n     7→export async function GET(\n     8→  _req: NextRequest,\n     9→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    10→) {\n    11→  const { id } = await params;\n    12→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    13→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    15→  }\n    16→\n    17→  const listing = db\n    18→    .prepare(\n    19→      `SELECT\n    20→        l.*,\n    21→        u.display_name as author_name\n    22→      FROM listings l\n    23→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    24→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    27→\n    28→  if (!listing) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  const members = db\n    33→    .prepare(\n    34→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    35→       FROM listing_members lm\n    36→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    37→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    38→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    39→    )\n    40→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    41→\n    42→  const session = await getSession();\n    43→  const isMember = session.userId\n    44→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    45→    : false;\n    46→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    47→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    48→    : false;\n    49→\n    50→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    51→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    52→    return NextResponse.json(\n    53→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    54→      { status: 404 }\n    55→    );\n    56→  }\n    57→\n    58→  // Check if the current user has a pending application\n    59→  let hasApplied = false;\n    60→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    61→  if (session.userId && !isMember && listing.requires_approval) {\n    62→    const app = db\n    63→      .prepare(\n    64→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    65→      )\n    66→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    67→    if (app) {\n    68→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    69→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    70→    }\n    71→  }\n    72→\n    73→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    74→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    75→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    76→    pendingApplicants = db\n    77→      .prepare(\n    78→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    79→         FROM listing_applications la\n    80→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    81→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    82→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    83→      )\n    84→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    85→  }\n    86→\n    87→  return NextResponse.json({\n    88→    listing: {\n    89→      ...listing,\n    90→      members,\n    91→      memberCount: members.length,\n    92→      isMember,\n    93→      isAuthor,\n    94→      hasApplied,\n    95→      applicationStatus,\n    96→      pendingApplicants,\n    97→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n    98→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    99→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   100→    },\n   101→  });\n   102→}\n   103→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"02efdb52-a6ff-4de9-a826-d16517f7bdd7","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:30.233Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NawpQ8uWmV217Ew4pg4CnG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Listing {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  book_title: string;\n     9→  book_author: string;\n    10→  book_cover_url: string;\n    11→  language: string;\n    12→  reading_pace: string;\n    13→  start_date: string;\n    14→  meeting_format: string;\n    15→  max_group_size: number;\n    16→ function HomePage() {\n    24→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    26→\n    27→  // Search & filter state\n    28→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    29→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    30→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    33→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    34→\n    35→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    36→\n    37→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    38→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string) => {\n    39→      setLoading(true);\n    40→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    41→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    42→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    43→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    44→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    45→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    46→\n    47→      const qs = params.toString();\n    48→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    49→        .then((r) => r.json())\n    50→        .then((data) => {\n    51→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    52→          setLoading(false);\n    53→        })\n    54→        .catch(() => setLoading(false));\n    55→    },\n    56→    []\n    57→  );\n    58→\n    59→  // Initial fetch\n    60→  useEffect(() => {\n    61→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n    62→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    63→\n    64→  // Debounced search\n    65→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    66→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    67→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    68→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    69→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    70→    }, 400);\n    71→  };\n    72→\n    73→  // Immediate filter changes\n    74→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    75→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    76→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    77→  };\n    78→\n    79→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n    80→    setReadingPace(value);\n    81→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom);\n    82→  };\n    83→\n    84→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n    86→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value);\n    87→  };\n    88→\n    89→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n    90→    setSort(value);\n    91→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom);\n    92→  };\n    93→\n    94→  const clearFilters = () => {\n    95→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n    96→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n    97→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n    98→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n    99→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   100→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\");\n   101→  };\n   102→\n   103→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   104→\n   105→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   106→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   107→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   108→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   109→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   110→    };\n   111→    return labels[f] || f;\n   112→  };\n   113→\n   114→  return (\n   115→    <div>\n   116→      <div className=\"text-center mb-8 mt-4\">\n   117→        <h1 className=\"text-4xl mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   118→          Find your reading companion\n   119→        </h1>\n   120→        <p\n   121→          className=\"text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   122→          style={{\n   123→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   124→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   125→          }}\n   126→        >\n   127→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   128→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   129→        </p>\n   130→      </div>\n   131→\n   132→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   133→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   134→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 author...\"\n   139→              value={searchQuery}\n   140→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   141→              className=\"input-field\"\n   142→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   143→            />\n   144→            <svg\n   145→              style={{\n   146→                position: \"absolute\",\n   147→                left: {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Dzpa1HpCn4BnYRggL2Uikc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→\n     5→interface BookResult {\n     6→  key: string;\n     7→  title: string;\n     8→  author_name?: string[];\n     9→  cover_i?: number;\n    10→}\n    11→\n    12→interface Props {\n    13→  onSelect: (book: {\n    14→    title: string;\n    15→    author: string;\n    16→    coverUrl: string;\n    17→    olid: string;\n    18→  }) => void;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    22→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    23→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    24→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    25→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    26→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    27→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  useEffect(() => {\n    30→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    31→      if (\n    32→        containerRef.current &&\n    33→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    34→      ) {\n    35→        setOpen(false);\n    36→      }\n    37→    };\n    38→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    39→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    40→  }, []);\n    41→\n    42→  useEffect(() => {\n    43→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    44→      setResults([]);\n    45→      return;\n    46→    }\n    47→\n    48→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    49→\n    50→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    51→      setLoading(true);\n    52→      try {\n    53→        const res = await fetch(\n    54→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`\n    55→        );\n    56→        const data = await res.json();\n    57→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    58→        setOpen(true);\n    59→      } catch {\n    60→        setResults([]);\n    61→      } finally {\n    62→        setLoading(false);\n    63→      }\n    64→    }, 400);\n    65→\n    66→    return () => {\n    67→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    68→    };\n    69→  }, [query]);\n    70→\n    71→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    72→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    73→    onSelect({\n    74→      title: book.title,\n    75→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    76→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    77→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    78→        : \"\",\n    79→      olid,\n    80→    });\n    81→    setQuery(book.title);\n    82→    setOpen(false);\n    83→  };\n    84→\n    85→  return (\n    86→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n    87→      <input\n    88→        type=\"text\"\n    89→        className=\"input-field\"\n    90→        value={query}\n    91→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n    92→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n    93→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n    94→      />\n    95→      {loading && (\n    96→        <div\n    97→          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n    98→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    99→        >\n   100→          Searching...\n   101→        </div>\n   102→      )}\n   103→\n   104→      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n   105→        <div\n   106→          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n   107→          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n   108→        >\n   109→          {results.map((book) => (\n   110→            <button\n   111→              key={book.key}\n   112→              type=\"button\"\n   113→              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n   114→              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n   115→            >\n   116→              {book.cover_i ? (\n   117→                <img\n   118→                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n   119→                  alt=\"\"\n   120→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n   121→                />\n   122→              ) : (\n   123→                <div\n   124→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n   125→                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n   126→                >\n   127→                  📖\n   128→                </div>\n   129→              )}\n   130→              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n   131→                <div\n   132→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n   133→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   134→                >\n   135→                  {book.title}\n   136→                </div>\n   137→                <div\n   138→                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n   139→                  style={{\n   140→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   141→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   142→                  }}\n   143→                >\n   144→                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n   145→                </div>\n   146→              </div>\n   147→            </button>\n   148→          ))}\n   149→        </div>\n   150→      )}\n   151→    </div>\n   152→  );\n   153→}\n   154→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"98aae69e-9432-4460-8e14-c5d7bf63dd2f","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:42.397Z"}

= await fetch(`/api/ratings?listingId=${id}`);\n   103→      const data = await res.json();\n   104→      if (data.givenRatings) {\n   105→        const given: Record<number, { score: number; comment: string }> = {};\n   106→        const scores: Record<number, number> = {};\n   107→        const comments: Record<number, string> = {};\n   108→        for (const r of data.givenRatings) {\n   109→ ratings are not critical\n   119→    }\n   120→  }, [id]);\n   121→\n   122→  useEffect(() => {\n   123→    if (listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember) {\n   124→      fetchRatings();\n   125→    }\n   126→  }, [listing?.is_full, listing?.isMember, fetchRatings]);\n   127→\n   128→  const handleRatingSubmit = async (memberId: number) => {\n   129→    const score = ratingScores[memberId];\n   130→    if (!score) return;\n   131→    setRatingSaving(memberId);\n   132→    setRatingMessage(\"\");\n   133→    try {\n   134→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/ratings\", {\n   135→        method: \"POST\",\n   136→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   137→        body: JSON.stringify({\n   138→          listingId: parseInt(id as string, 10),\n   139→          ratedUserId: memberId,\n   140→          score,\n   141→          comment: ratingComments[memberId] || \"\",\n   142→        }),\n   143→      });\n   144→      const data = await res.json();\n   145→      if (!res.ok) {\n   146→        setRatingMessage(data.error || \"Failed to submit rating\");\n   147→      } else {\n   148→        setRatingMessage(\"Rating saved!\");\n   149→        setGivenRatings((prev) => ({\n   150→          ...prev,\n   151→          [memberId]: { score, comment: ratingComments[memberId] || \"\" },\n   152→        }));\n   153→      }\n   154→    } catch {\n   155→      setRatingMessage(\"Failed to submit rating\");\n   156→    } finally {\n   157→      setRatingSaving(null);\n   158→    }\n   159→  };\n   160→\n   161→  const handleJoin = async () => {\n   162→    setJoining(true);\n   163→    setError(\"\");\n   164→    try {\n   165→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/join`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   166→      const data = await res.json();\n   167→      if (!res.ok) {\n   168→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   169→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   170→          return;\n   171→        }\n   172→        setError(data.error);\n   173→        return;\n   174→      }\n   175→      await fetchListing();\n   176→    } catch {\n   177→      setError(\"Failed to join. Please try again.\");\n   178→    } finally {\n   179→      setJoining(false);\n   180→    }\n   181→  };\n   182→\n   183→  const handleApply = async () => {\n   184→    setApplying(true);\n   185→    setError(\"\");\n   186→    try {\n   187→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/apply`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   188→      const data = await res.json();\n   189→      if (!res.ok) {\n   190→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   191→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   192→          return;\n   193→        }\n   194→        setError(data.error);\n   195→        return;\n   196→      }\n   197→      await fetchListing();\n   198→    } catch {\n   199→      setError(\"Failed to apply. Please try again.\");\n   200→    } finally {\n   201→      setApplying(false);\n   202→    }\n   203→  };\n   204→\n   205→  const handleDecision = async (applicationId: number, action: \"approve\" | \"reject\") => {\n   206→    setDecidingId(applicationId);\n   207→    setError(\"\");\n   208→    try {\n   209→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/applications`, {\n   210→        method: \"PATCH\",\n   211→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   212→        body: JSON.stringify({ applicationId, action }),\n   213→      });\n   214→      const data = await res.json();\n   215→      if (!res.ok) {\n   216→        setError(data.error);\n   217→        return;\n   218→      }\n   219→      await fetchListing();"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_016KRVynExK2khS6vbSRMzhc","session_id":"56b902f7-a667-4b55-8104-777174b59df9","uuid":"6505569d-351e-46f5-94a3-2108dbdc95ce","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:47.045Z"}
